_meta.title: BRSR ↔ ESRS (CSRD) indicative crosswalk
_meta.framework: European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS), the reporting standards behind the EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD). Delegated Regulation (EU) 2023/2772.
standards: ESRS E3 Water and marine resources
ESRS E5 Resource use and circular economy
ESRS S2 Workers in the value chain
_meta.note: Indicative mapping of BRSR disclosures to the ESRS topical standard that asks for the same information. A consultant aid, not an official SEBI/EFRAG crosswalk. Sparse by design: a blank means we are not claiming a clean counterpart, not that the disclosure is irrelevant. Some BRSR disclosures are India-specific (PAT, statutory CSR spend) and have no ESRS equivalent, which is stated in the detail rather than papered over.
_meta.scope_caveat: This is a crosswalk, not an applicability test. Whether a company must report under CSRD depends on EU turnover and headcount thresholds that this tool does not collect, so no applicability verdict is offered.
_meta.omnibus_status: Omnibus I (Directive (EU) 2025/794) entered into force 18 March 2026 and narrowed CSRD scope to undertakings with more than 1,000 employees and turnover above EUR 450 million. The revised ESRS cut mandatory datapoints by roughly 60-70%, with adoption targeted around September 2026 and application from FY2027 (FY2026 voluntary). The twelve standards and their topics are unchanged, so a topic-level crosswalk holds; specific datapoint references may move.

sources.label: EFRAG / European Commission, ESRS Delegated Regulation (EU) 2023/2772

P1-G1.detail: Role of the administrative, management and supervisory bodies in overseeing sustainability matters (ESRS 2 GOV-1)

P1-G2.detail: Information provided to and sustainability matters addressed by the governance bodies (ESRS 2 GOV-2)

P1-G4.detail: Business conduct policies and prevention and detection of corruption and bribery (ESRS G1-1, G1-3)

P1-G5.detail: Business conduct policies and corporate culture, including conflicts of interest (ESRS G1-1)

P1-G6.detail: Training on the prevention and detection of corruption and bribery (ESRS G1-3)

P1-G7.detail: Interests and views of stakeholders, and channels to raise concerns (ESRS 2 SBM-2; ESRS G1-1 whistleblower protection)

P1-G8.detail: General basis for preparation of the sustainability statement (ESRS 2 BP-1, BP-2)

P2-S2.detail: Resource inflows, including the share of sustainably sourced materials (ESRS E5-4)

P2-S3.detail: Resource outflows and products at end of life; EPR is the Indian instrument, ESRS asks for the underlying circularity data (ESRS E5-5)

P2-S4.detail: Circular product design and life-cycle impacts, which LCA evidences (ESRS E5-1 policies, E5-4 and E5-5 inflows and outflows)

P2-S5.detail: Resource outflows, reclaimed and recycled content in products sold (ESRS E5-5)

P3-L1.detail: Characteristics of the undertaking's employees and of non-employee workers (ESRS S1-6, S1-7)

P3-L2.detail: Diversity metrics, gender distribution across the workforce and at top management (ESRS S1-9)

P3-L3.detail: Persons with disabilities in the workforce (ESRS S1-12)

P3-L4.detail: Employee turnover, disclosed within employee characteristics (ESRS S1-6)

P3-L5.detail: Remuneration metrics, gender pay gap and total compensation ratio (ESRS S1-16)

P3-L6.detail: Family-related leave metrics, including return to work (ESRS S1-15)

P3-L7.detail: Health and safety management system coverage (ESRS S1-14)

P3-L8.detail: Recordable work-related injuries, fatalities and rates (ESRS S1-14)

P3-L9.detail: Cases of recordable work-related ill health (ESRS S1-14)

P3-L10.detail: Training and skills development metrics, including average training hours (ESRS S1-13)

P3-L11.detail: Social protection coverage against major life events (ESRS S1-11)

P3-L12.detail: Collective bargaining coverage and social dialogue (ESRS S1-8)

P3-L13.detail: Complaints channels and incidents relating to working conditions (ESRS S1-3, S1-17)

P3-L14.detail: Adequate wages, share of employees paid at or above the applicable benchmark (ESRS S1-10)

P4-Q1.detail: Identification of affected stakeholders and users of the statement (ESRS 2 SBM-2)

P4-Q2.detail: How and how often the undertaking engages with stakeholders (ESRS 2 SBM-2)

P4-Q3.detail: Material impacts, risks and opportunities identified through the double-materiality assessment (ESRS 2 IRO-1, SBM-3)

P5-H1.detail: Human rights policy commitments and training for the workforce (ESRS S1-1); value-chain training feeds ESRS S2-1

P5-H3.detail: Incidents, complaints and severe human rights impacts, including discrimination and harassment (ESRS S1-17)

P5-H4.detail: Human rights due diligence across the value chain (ESRS S2-1, S2-2; ESRS 2 GOV-4 due-diligence statement)

P5-H5.detail: Processes to remediate negative impacts and channels to raise concerns (ESRS S1-3; S2-3 for value-chain workers)

P6-E1.detail: Energy consumption and mix, electricity purchased (ESRS E1-5)

P6-E2.detail: Energy consumption from fossil sources, disaggregated by fuel (ESRS E1-5)

P6-E3.detail: Energy consumption from nuclear and renewable sources (ESRS E1-5)

P6-E4.detail: Total energy consumption and energy intensity per net revenue (ESRS E1-5)

P6-E5.detail: Share of renewable sources in total energy consumption (ESRS E1-5)

P6-E6.detail: Energy-efficiency actions and resources (ESRS E1-3). PAT is an India-specific trading scheme with no direct ESRS counterpart; the underlying energy data is what transfers.

P6-E7.detail: Water withdrawals, including withdrawals in areas of water stress (ESRS E3-4)

P6-E8.detail: Water consumption and water intensity per net revenue (ESRS E3-4)

P6-E9.detail: Water discharges, disclosed within water consumption metrics (ESRS E3-4)

P6-E10.detail: Actions and resources related to water, including discharge reduction (ESRS E3-2). Discharge quality also feeds ESRS E2-4 water pollution.

P6-E12.detail: Gross Scope 2 GHG emissions, location-based and market-based (ESRS E1-6)

P6-E13.detail: Gross Scope 3 GHG emissions by category (ESRS E1-6). Mandatory under ESRS where material, unlike BRSR where Scope 3 is a Leadership indicator.

P6-E14.detail: GHG intensity per net revenue (ESRS E1-6)

P6-E15.detail: Targets related to climate change mitigation and adaptation (ESRS E1-4)

P6-E16.detail: GHG removals and mitigation projects financed through carbon credits (ESRS E1-7)

P6-E17.detail: Resource outflows, total waste generated by hazardous and non-hazardous (ESRS E5-5)

P6-E18.detail: Waste diverted from disposal by recovery operation (ESRS E5-5)

P6-E19.detail: Waste directed to disposal by disposal operation (ESRS E5-5)

P6-E20.detail: Pollution of air, water and soil, emissions of pollutants to air (ESRS E2-4)

P6-E21.detail: Process to identify and assess material environmental impacts, risks and opportunities (ESRS 2 IRO-1)

P6-E22.detail: Sites located in or near biodiversity-sensitive areas and impact metrics (ESRS E4-5; ESRS 2 SBM-3 for material impacts)

P6-E23.detail: Actions and resources related to resource use and circular economy, including plastics (ESRS E5-2)

P6-E25.detail: Anticipated financial effects from material physical and transition risks (ESRS E1-9; ESRS 2 SBM-3)

P6-E26.detail: Resilience of the strategy tested through climate scenario analysis (ESRS E1 SBM-3; E1-1 transition plan)

P6-E27.detail: Internal carbon pricing schemes and how they are applied (ESRS E1-8)

P7-R1.detail: Political influence and lobbying activities, including trade-association memberships (ESRS G1-5)

P7-R2.detail: Business conduct policies and corporate culture (ESRS G1-1). ESRS has no dedicated anti-competitive-conduct datapoint.

P7-R3.detail: Political influence and lobbying, including positions taken and their alignment with material impacts (ESRS G1-5)

P8-I1.detail: Affected communities (ESRS S3). ESRS has no CSR-spend datapoint; mandatory CSR spend is India-specific (Companies Act s.135), so the spend figure does not transfer, the community impact behind it does.

P8-I2.detail: Workers in the value chain (ESRS S2); payment practices toward smaller suppliers feed ESRS G1-6

P8-I3.detail: Value-chain workers and supplier practices, procurement from vulnerable groups (ESRS S2-1, S2-4)

P8-I4.detail: Taking action on material impacts on affected communities (ESRS S3-4)

P8-I5.detail: Material impacts on affected communities, including economic impacts of operations (ESRS S3 SBM-3, S3-4)

P9-C1.detail: Channels for consumers and end-users to raise concerns, and remediation (ESRS S4-3)

P9-C2.detail: Personal safety of consumers and end-users, and actions taken (ESRS S4 SBM-3, S4-4)

P9-C3.detail: Information-related impacts for consumers, privacy and data protection (ESRS S4-1, S4-4)

P9-C4.detail: Policies protecting consumer information and privacy (ESRS S4-1)

P9-C5.detail: Access to quality information and responsible marketing to consumers (ESRS S4-1, S4-4)

P9-C6.detail: Processes for engaging with consumers and end-users about impacts (ESRS S4-2)

P9-C7.detail: Personal safety and informed use by consumers and end-users (ESRS S4-1, S4-4)
